shared_properties: The student duelist keeps the academy coat silhouette, body proportion, and lower-lane anchor established by player_duelist_idle_loop.
Core palette metadata remains primary #5C3317, background #2B1B17, and accent #D4AF37 while success energy adds cyan and warm white in specs.colors_hex.
Hand, wand, and card fan landmarks stay consistent so the state transition remains readable inside one combat beat.
differences: Six frames show a committed counter release with the wand sweeping upward and a crescent reflection arc leaving the player toward the enemy lane.
The off-hand card flashes gold-white to sell a successful timing confirmation.
Cape tails flare outward and the torso twists slightly so the state reads as an active payoff rather than a defensive hold.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the duel loop, projectile attributes, UI layout, and Bevy runtime constraints for Reflect Academy.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the warm academy hall mood, layered duel composition, and luminous spell contrast.

reference_images.label: player_concept
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the student duelist silhouette, costume trim, wand handling, and heroic stance.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load player_duelist_reflect_success.png and build a 3x2 atlas for the short payoff animation played immediately after a successful reflect.
runtime_notes: Play once at 12 FPS, then transition back to the idle or ready atlas based on the next projectile state.
Keep the root transform on the same duel floor anchor so downstream hit sparks and popup UI align correctly.
The authored arc should remain readable even when camera shake amplitude reaches 8 px during a large counter.

context.usage: Displayed for the short reward beat immediately after a successful reflect sends the spell back toward the enemy.

effect_notes: The counter arc exits the wand tip and curves toward the upper-right lane.
Gold-white confirmation flash should occupy less than 20% of the frame area.
The cape flare should never extend beyond the 160 px frame width.

acceptance_criteria: Atlas canvas is exactly 480x400 px for six 160x200 frames with no frame gutter.
The reflected arc direction is unambiguous toward the upper-right in every frame.
The success pose remains visually distinct from the ready pose when both sheets are previewed side by side.
